raw_response: {"rating": 4.5, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is a news report that adopts strongly pro-life framing throughout the author's own prose, consistently using language and characterizations aligned with right-leaning, anti-abortion values. The author's word choices, sourcing structure, and editorial framing all point in one ideological direction, warranting a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author uses the phrase 'unborn babies' in their own voice — a term preferred by pro-life advocates rather than the neutral 'fetus' or 'embryo' — signaling ideological alignment before any sources are quoted; (2) The author refers to pro-abortion ballot measures as 'radical measures' in their own connecting prose, adopting oppositional framing rather than neutral description; (3) The author describes abortion-supporting ballot measures as backed by 'billionaires with eugenicist leanings' — a charged characterization presented as a factual editorial claim, not attributed to any source; (4) The article's sourcing structure is entirely one-sided: every named organization or individual platformed belongs to the pro-life movement (Heartbeat International, Live Action, SBA Pro-Life America), with no pro-choice voices, neutral policy analysts, or opposing perspectives represented anywhere in the piece; (5) The author describes pro-abortion spending as 'out-of-state dark money' and frames amendment campaigns as manipulation of voters, using adversarial language that mirrors pro-life advocacy rhetoric rather than neutral journalistic description; (6) The embedded section header 'Left-Wing Coalition Spent at Least $110 Million on Failed Florida Abortion Ballot Measure' is an editorial choice that frames abortion-rights supporters as a partisan bloc, reinforcing a right-leaning narrative frame."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report that adopts strongly pro-life framing throughout the author's own prose, consistently using language and characterizations aligned with right-leaning, anti-abortion values. The author's word choices, sourcing structure, and editorial framing all point in one ideological direction, warranting a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author uses the phrase 'unborn babies' in their own voice — a term preferred by pro-life advocates rather than the neutral 'fetus' or 'embryo' — signaling ideological alignment before any sources are quoted; (2) The author refers to pro-abortion ballot measures as 'radical measures' in their own connecting prose, adopting oppositional framing rather than neutral description; (3) The author describes abortion-supporting ballot measures as backed by 'billionaires with eugenicist leanings' — a charged characterization presented as a factual editorial claim, not attributed to any source; (4) The article's sourcing structure is entirely one-sided: every named organization or individual platformed belongs to the pro-life movement (Heartbeat International, Live Action, SBA Pro-Life America), with no pro-choice voices, neutral policy analysts, or opposing perspectives represented anywhere in the piece; (5) The author describes pro-abortion spending as 'out-of-state dark money' and frames amendment campaigns as manipulation of voters, using adversarial language that mirrors pro-life advocacy rhetoric rather than neutral journalistic description; (6) The embedded section header 'Left-Wing Coalition Spent at Least $110 Million on Failed Florida Abortion Ballot Measure' is an editorial choice that frames abortion-rights supporters as a partisan bloc, reinforcing a right-leaning narrative frame.

explanation: This is a news report that adopts strongly pro-life framing throughout the author's own prose, consistently using language and characterizations aligned with right-leaning, anti-abortion values. The author's word choices, sourcing structure, and editorial framing all point in one ideological direction, warranting a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author uses the phrase 'unborn babies' in their own voice — a term preferred by pro-life advocates rather than the neutral 'fetus' or 'embryo' — signaling ideological alignment before any sources are quoted; (2) The author refers to pro-abortion ballot measures as 'radical measures' in their own connecting prose, adopting oppositional framing rather than neutral description; (3) The author describes abortion-supporting ballot measures as backed by 'billionaires with eugenicist leanings' — a charged characterization presented as a factual editorial claim, not attributed to any source; (4) The article's sourcing structure is entirely one-sided: every named organization or individual platformed belongs to the pro-life movement (Heartbeat International, Live Action, SBA Pro-Life America), with no pro-choice voices, neutral policy analysts, or opposing perspectives represented anywhere in the piece; (5) The author describes pro-abortion spending as 'out-of-state dark money' and frames amendment campaigns as manipulation of voters, using adversarial language that mirrors pro-life advocacy rhetoric rather than neutral journalistic description; (6) The embedded section header 'Left-Wing Coalition Spent at Least $110 Million on Failed Florida Abortion Ballot Measure' is an editorial choice that frames abortion-rights supporters as a partisan bloc, reinforcing a right-leaning narrative frame.
